title: FR: Make sorting connections reversible
author: rubaboo
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->
<!-- If you file a feature request, please delete the bug section -->
It's easy to screw up a carefully ordered list of connections by hitting the sort button. Please implement one of these (in order of preference):
1. make the sort button into a switch: down = sorted, up = custom ordered
2. an option to disable the sort connections functionality
3. a confirmation dialog: "Sort connections? There is no undo."

iterations.description: Users currently lose manual connection ordering when sorting; an undo or toggle is needed to restore the previous state.

comments.author: janborup
comments.snippet: In the version 1.75.7000 it was easy to drag-n-drop sorting, but this feature does not work anymore.

comments.author: sparerd
comments.snippet: @janborup Thanks for reporting that, i've opened #814 for that bug

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Users currently lose manual connection ordering when sorting; an undo or toggle is needed to restore the previous state.
Approach: Implement an 'Undo Sort' feature by caching the child node order of a folder before applying a sort operation, allowing restoration of the manual order.
